title: News sites are the new newspapers: People are abandoning them for social media
tldr: A new global study finds people across all age groups—especially 18-34s—are deserting publisher sites and apps for social and video platforms, mirroring the decline of print. A structural shift in how audiences find news.
angle: What the death of the publisher homepage means for owned-media strategy and where content strategists should place their bets next.

title: Your Best-Ranked Page Might Be Invisible to Google's AI
tldr: Top-10 Google rankings no longer guarantee visibility because AI Overviews cite different sources. SEO success metrics need rethinking in the GEO era.
angle: Why content strategists must shift from ranking to being cited—and how to audit for AI-Overview invisibility.

title: The #1 Role Your Content Team Needs in 2026 Is a Managing Editor
tldr: As AI makes production cheap, editorial judgment becomes the differentiator—arguing every content team needs a managing editor. A clear stance on what humans should own.
why_fresh: new org-design argument for AI era
angle: Editorial judgment as the new competitive moat: redesigning content teams when AI handles the drafting.

title: LinkedIn's Algorithm Killed Company Pages. Employees Are The Distribution Now.
tldr: LinkedIn's algorithm shift has gutted brand-page reach, pushing companies toward employee-generated content as their primary distribution channel. Direct relevance to internal comms and EGC.
angle: Building an employee-generated content engine now that company pages are dead distribution.

title: The rise of 'clipping' on social media
tldr: PR's new 'clipping' means slicing long videos into short clips—raising disclosure and rights questions. A practical short-form content trend with compliance implications.
angle: The clipping economy: how to repurpose long video ethically and at scale without disclosure landmines.

title: Building a tier-1 media map for new media
tldr: With 17,000+ media jobs cut in 2025, 'tier-1' media targets are being redefined to include creators, newsletters and niche outlets. A rethink of media relations for a contracting-and-expanding landscape.
angle: Rebuilding your media map for an era where a newsletter writer outranks a legacy masthead.

title: The Future of Content Belongs to the Tastemakers
tldr: With AI making polished copy trivial, taste and curation become the scarce, defining skills for content teams. A thesis on where human value concentrates.
angle: Curation as craft: why taste, not output, is the content strategist's defensible asset.

title: What kind of stories turn local news readers into subscribers? Hard news, not soft stuff
tldr: New research shows hard news—government, public health, politics—drives subscriptions far more than soft lifestyle content. Counters the clickbait-for-conversion assumption.
angle: What subscription data teaches content strategists about value vs. engagement bait.

title: A 6-step framework for boosting social GEO
tldr: A practical framework for creating social content that AI platforms want to cite, as consumers increasingly use AI to research. Actionable GEO playbook.
angle: Optimizing social content to be cited by AI: a hands-on GEO workflow for comms teams.

title: The Anti-AI Brand Is Becoming a Real Market Position
tldr: Brands are starting to differentiate by openly rejecting AI, turning 'human-made' into a positioning play. A genuinely new branding angle.
angle: 'Made by humans' as a brand differentiator: when anti-AI becomes a corporate narrative.

title: Why 'AI Productivity Gains' Is the Wrong Pitch for Every Stakeholder Above You
tldr: Pitching AI as productivity won't persuade your CMO, CFO or legal—each needs a reframed metric to unlock budget and protect headcount. Practical internal-influence guidance.
why_fresh: reframes AI business case per stakeholder
angle: Stakeholder-tailored framing: how to sell content/AI investment up the chain without the productivity cliché.

title: WPP forecasts AI search ads to become fastest growing channel in advertising
tldr: WPP and Madison & Wall project AI-search advertising as the fastest-growing ad channel amid a resilient ad economy. Signals where paid media spend is heading.
angle: Preparing content and paid strategy for the AI-search ad era.

title: Hooked on the Scroll: How Vertical Dramas are Changing Brand Storytelling
tldr: Vertical micro-dramas are emerging as a new format reshaping how brands tell stories on mobile. A fresh short-form narrative trend.
angle: Can brands tell real stories in vertical micro-drama episodes? Lessons from the format's rise.

title: LinkedIn Invests in B2B Creator Economy with Launch of Creator Marketplace
tldr: LinkedIn launched a Creator Marketplace built specifically for B2B, formalizing creator partnerships for business brands. A structural move for B2B content distribution.
angle: What a native B2B creator marketplace means for thought leadership and earned-creator strategy.

title: Эпоха легкого заработка на маркетплейсах закончилась. Бренды уходят.
tldr: Sellers are leaving Wildberries and Ozon as new regulation and thin margins bite, pushing small brands to find customers elsewhere. A shift in where brands build audiences.
angle: When marketplaces stop working: how brands rebuild owned audiences and direct relationships.
